channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Virginia’s Gerrymandering Vandalism
item.description: The Virginia gerrymander would create an extreme deep-blue House caucus in a purple state based upon winning one election cycle.

item.title: Democrats Want To Stop Illegal Alien Deportations By Shutting Down DHS. It’s That Simple
item.description: Just like the last time Democrats threw the government into a shutdown, they have no leverage but are now withholding votes to fund the Department of ...

item.title: A Congressman’s Five Questions for Munich
item.description: This week’s Munich Security Conference comes at a consequential moment for both U.S. security planning and the future of the Western alliance.

item.title: Trump invites Latin America leaders to attend summit in Florida
item.description: President Trump has invited Latin American leaders to attend a summit in Florida next month in a bid to counter China's expanding influence in the reg...

item.title: Crypto lender BlockFills suspends withdrawals for clients in latest blow to the blockchain sector
item.description: During the digital assets crash of 2022, lenders made similarly protective moves.

item.title: ESPN’s Philly Special Documents The Single Play That United A City
item.description: 'Philly Special' provides new context for die-hard Eagles fans — and fans of comeback, underdog stories.

item.title: Barbados' prime minister clinches a third term in office after a clean sweep at the polls
item.description: Barbados Prime Minister Mia Mottley has clinched a third consecutive term in office after her party secured all 30 seats of Parliament in this week's ...

item.title: Billionaire founder of Minecraft slams anyone advocating using AI to write code as ‘incompetent or evil’
item.description: Minecraft creator Markus Persson has just issued a warning: That AI is “being forced down our throats” and that leaders behind the push are “evil.”

item.title: US lawmakers warn Taiwan to ‘meet the moment’ as China stages invasion-style drills
item.description: Bipartisan U.S. lawmakers warn Taiwan's political gridlock over defense spending could undermine deterrence as China escalates military pressure.

item.title: High Fashion Wasn’t Easy in Imperial Spain
item.description: Fabrics, jewelry, and tailoring were tops, but the Hispanic Society shows us that looking good could be hazardous to your health.

item.title: Instagram boss reveals he’s paid $900k per year plus stock worth ‘tens of millions of dollars’ as he denies ‘addiction’ claims
item.description: The plaintiff’s lawyer in a landmark social media addiction trial tried to connect Adam Mosseri’s compensation with Instagram’s policy toward filters.

item.title: Nicki Minaj urges fans to call on Senators to pass SAVE Act
item.description: Rapper Nicki Minaj is coaxing her fans into calling their legislators to urge them to pass a bill requiring documentation of U.S. citizenship to vote.

item.title: In Its Latest Act of Repression, Hong Kong Convicted My Father for My Activism
item.description: My father’s conviction is Beijing’s litmus test for Washington: Will the U.S. stand firm in favor of freedom?

item.title: Federal judge upholds temporary protected status for Haitian immigrants
item.description: A federal judge upheld temporary protected status for Haitian immigrants in the United States and quoted death threats she's received, during a Thursd...

item.title: One of Wall Street’s most feared hedge fund managers on the decline of the dollar: gold is ‘becoming the reserve asset’
item.description: Greenlight Capital's David Einhorn, known for his “Einhorn Effect” and years of touting gold, says the world's central banks don't love dollars anymor...

item.title: OpenClaw is the bad boy of AI agents. Here’s why security experts say you should beware
item.description: The open-source AI agent pushes autonomy to the edge — thrilling hobbyists and unnerving security experts

item.title: Surrogacy Left These 21 Children Parentless After Alleged Abuse. Until It’s Banned, They Won’t Be The Last
item.description: No amount of regulation can fix a system that is predicated on encroaching on a child’s natural right to his biological mother and father.

item.title: Federal judge blocks Pentagon from demoting Mark Kelly over controversial military video
item.description: A federal judge blocked the Pentagon from punishing Sen. Mark Kelly, D-Ariz., over a controversial video, ruling his First Amendment rights were viola...

item.title: The ICE Drawdown In Minneapolis Is Probably A Surge To California
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security has been rapidly growing its footprint across the country, but California is high on the list of new and expanded ...

item.title: Judge orders ICE to let clergy distribute ashes to migrants in detention on Ash Wednesday
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday ordered ICE to let members of the clergy into a migrant processing facility near Chicago to distribute ashes next week on ...

item.title: Federal judge blocks Trump's bid to move Biden-commuted death row inmates to ‘Alcatraz of the Rockies’
item.description: A judge who blocked the Trump administration from transferring former death row inmates said the criminals were convicted of "some of the most horrifi...

item.title: Trump Busts Another Taboo
item.description: It used to be that U.S. presidents didn’t pop off on every Tom, Dick, and Harry.

item.title: 'Betrayed by their country': House Democrats hear sympathetic voices of DHS' deportation targets
item.description: Democratic lawmakers on Thursday bolstered their demand for stricter rules for ICE agents by hosting advocates for illegal immigrants and people caugh...

item.title: WV Lawmakers Launch Freedom Caucus To Take On ‘Undocumented Democrats’ In The GOP
item.description: 'It's fitting that we are launching the West Virginia Freedom Caucus on the 250th anniversary [of our nation] to restore those same rights to West Vir...

item.title: Leavitt unloads on Obama over voter ID push, accuses Dems of 'panic'
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt criticizes former President Barack Obama for opposing voter ID laws after House passes SAVE Act.

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Bank Risk
item.description: AI is exposing the limits of legacy bank controls, making governance quality critical to resilience and investor confidence. The post AI Is Reshaping ...

item.title: What Makes an Ideal Leveraged Buyout Candidate?
item.description: In competitive PE markets, successful LBOs depend on durable business models, recurring cash flows, defensible market positions, and disciplined targe...

item.title: Rethinking Household Asset Allocation Under Capital Constraints
item.description: Leveraged ETFs aren’t about more risk, they’re about using less capital to achieve a desired risk profile in household portfolios. The post Rethinking...

item.title: Stockholm’s Capital Markets Success: More Than Meatballs
item.description: Stockholm’s capital-market success reflects more than IPOs and PE activity. It offers a case study in how culture, institutions, and incentives shape ...
